title: Franca nodiflora
taxonomy: Franca nodiflora is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant is also known as the 'sensitive plant' due to its unique compound leaves. It is a member of the genus Franca and is closely related to other Apocynaceae species. Franca nodiflora is a flowering plant that is often found in humid and subtropical regions.

intro: Franca nodiflora, also known as 'wild chamomile', is a flowering plant species native to Africa, characterized by its small white flowers and compact growth habit. The plant is valued for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Franca nodiflora include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Franca nodiflora is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
